flashcards.front: What is a linear transformation T: V→W?
flashcards.back: A function that satisfies T(u+v) = T(u)+T(v) and T(av) = aT(v) for all u,v in V and scalar a.

flashcards.front: When is a linear transformation T: V→W invertible?
flashcards.back: T is invertible (nonsingular) if it is both one-to-one (injective) and onto (surjective), meaning it has an inverse T⁻¹ such that T⁻¹(T(v)) = v.

flashcards.front: What is the relationship between one-to-one and null space?
flashcards.back: A linear transformation is one-to-one if and only if its null space contains only the zero vector (ker(T) = {0}).
For T: V→W, dim(V) = rank(T) + nullity(T), where rank(T) = dim(range(T)) and nullity(T) = dim(null(T)).

flashcards.front: When is a square matrix A invertible?
flashcards.back: A square matrix A is invertible iff det(A) ≠ 0, or equivalently, its rank equals n (full rank), or all its eigenvalues are nonzero.

flashcards.front: What is the inverse of a 2×2 matrix [a b; c d]?
flashcards.back: The inverse is (1/det) × [d -b; -c a] where det = ad - bc ≠ 0.

flashcards.front: If T is invertible, what is det(T⁻¹)?
What is the Inverse of a Product Theorem?
flashcards.back: For invertible matrices/transformations A and B: (AB)⁻¹ = B⁻¹A⁻¹ (order reverses).

flashcards.front: How does invertibility relate to eigenvalues?
flashcards.back: A linear transformation is invertible if and only if none of its eigenvalues are zero.

flashcards.front: What is a one-to-one linear transformation?
flashcards.back: T is one-to-one if T(u) = T(v) implies u = v for all u,v in V (ker(T) = {0}).

flashcards.front: What is an onto (surjective) linear transformation?
flashcards.back: T is onto if for every w in W, there exists v in V such that T(v) = w (range(T) = W).

flashcards.front: What is the matrix representation of T⁻¹?
flashcards.back: If [T] is the matrix of T, then [T⁻¹] = [T]⁻¹ (the matrix inverse).

flashcards.front: What is the dimension requirement for invertibility?
flashcards.back: For T: V→V (square case), T is invertible iff rank(T) = dim(V).

flashcards.front: Define null space and range of a linear transformation
flashcards.back: Null space: {v ∈ V | T(v) = 0}; Range: {w ∈ W | w = T(v) for some v ∈ V}.

flashcards.front: What is the transpose of an invertible matrix?
flashcards.back: If A is invertible, then Aᵀ is also invertible and (Aᵀ)⁻¹ = (A⁻¹)ᵀ.
